event_handler_guidance.overview: BAW Coach Widgets support five lifecycle events that can be handled through individual JavaScript files in the widget/events/ folder. Event handlers are OPTIONAL - only create them when the widget's functionality requires specific event handling logic.

event.name: load
event.timing: Executed when the widget is first loaded and initialized

common_uses.use: Register custom event handlers with bpmext.ui.registerEventHandlingFunction()
Perform initial data processing or rendering
Set up timers, intervals, or external listeners

event.when_to_create: Create load.js when the widget needs initialization logic beyond what's in inlineJavascript.js, or when it needs to register custom BAW event handlers.

event.name: change
event.timing: Executed when the widget's bound data changes

common_uses.use: Update widget display based on new data
Fire custom events to notify other components

event.when_to_create: Create change.js when the widget needs to respond to data changes and update its display. This is one of the most commonly used event handlers.

event.name: view
event.timing: Executed when the widget becomes visible (e.g., tab switch, visibility toggle)

common_uses.use: Refresh widget display when it becomes visible

event.when_to_create: Create view.js when the widget needs to refresh or update when it becomes visible, especially for widgets in tabs or collapsible sections.

event.name: validate
event.timing: Executed during form validation (e.g., before form submission)

common_uses.use: Validate widget data against business rules

event.when_to_create: Create validate.js when the widget needs custom validation logic beyond standard HTML5 validation.

event.name: unload
event.timing: Executed when the widget is being removed or destroyed
event.when_to_create: Create unload.js when the widget creates resources that need explicit cleanup (timers, listeners, external connections).

decision_guide.question: Does the widget need initialization logic or custom event registration?
Does the widget need to update its display when data changes?
Does the widget need to refresh when becoming visible?
Does the widget need custom validation logic?
Does the widget create resources that need cleanup?

example.rationale: Needs load.js to register custom event handlers with bpmext.ui.registerEventHandlingFunction(). Needs change.js to re-render the task list when data changes.
Only needs change.js to update the breadcrumb navigation when the path changes. No initialization or cleanup required.
Only needs change.js to re-parse and render markdown when content changes. Simple display widget with no special lifecycle needs.
Simple display widget that formats dates. All logic is in inlineJavascript.js. No event handlers needed - the events/ folder exists but is empty.

practice.rule: Only create event handlers that the widget actually needs
practice.rationale: Unnecessary event handlers add complexity and maintenance burden without providing value. Start with no event handlers and add them only when functionality requires them.

practice.rule: Always use var _this = this; at the start of event handlers
practice.rationale: Ensures consistent access to the widget context across all event handler code. This is the BAW convention used in all existing widgets.

practice.rule: Keep event handlers focused on their specific lifecycle concern
practice.rationale: load.js for initialization, change.js for data updates, etc. Don't mix concerns across event handlers.

practice.rule: Use console.log() for debugging but consider removing in production
practice.rationale: Helpful during development but can clutter the console in production environments.

practice.rule: Document complex event handler logic with comments
practice.rationale: Event handlers can contain complex business logic that benefits from explanation.

pattern.name: data_driven_rendering
pattern.description: Widget updates its display based on data changes
pattern.example: Most list-based widgets (TasksList, Breadcrumb) follow this pattern. The change.js handler gets the new data and updates the DOM accordingly.

pattern.name: custom_event_integration
pattern.description: Widget registers and responds to custom BAW events
pattern.example: TasksList uses load.js to register "UpdateEvent" with bpmext.ui.registerEventHandlingFunction(). This allows the widget to respond to custom events from other components.

pattern.name: resource_lifecycle
pattern.description: Widget creates resources that need cleanup
pattern.example: A widget that creates timers in load.js should clear them in unload.js. A widget that adds event listeners should remove them in unload.js.

pattern.name: visibility_dependent
pattern.description: Widget needs to refresh when becoming visible
pattern.example: Widgets in tabs or collapsible sections that need to recalculate layout or refresh content when the user switches to their tab.

pattern.name: form_validation
pattern.example: Input widgets that need to validate data format, required fields, or business rules before form submission.

create_widget_template.description: The create_widget_template.py script supports optional event handler generation via the --events command-line flag.

usage.result: Creates widget with empty events/ folder
Creates widget with load.js and change.js event handlers
Creates widget with all five event handlers
